---
title: 'Data Analysis Using R'
author: "Saldi"
date: "2024-10-10"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## List of Library Used
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(cluster)
library(caret)
library(rpart)
library(pheatmap)
library(ggplot2)
library(factoextra)
library(rpart.plot)
```


## Checking Data Characteristic
```{r}
data <- read.csv("demo_influencer_campaign_dataset.csv")

str(data)
```
## Check Missing Values and Duplicate
```{r}
# Check for missing values in each column
missing_values <- sapply(data, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
print(missing_values)

# Check for duplicate rows
duplicate_rows <- data[duplicated(data), ]
print(nrow(duplicate_rows))
```
## Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
### Data Correlation
```{r}
# Select numeric columns for correlation analysis and rename them for better readability
numeric_data <- data %>%
  select(cmp_total_engagement, cmp_campaign_cost, inf_follower_count, 
         inf_engagement_rate, inf_post_frequency,  
         inf_engagement_growth_rate, inf_average_reach) %>%
  rename(
    `Campaign Engagement` = cmp_total_engagement,
    `Campaign Cost` = cmp_campaign_cost,
    `Influencer Followers` = inf_follower_count,
    `Influencer Engagement Rate` = inf_engagement_rate,
    `Influencer Post Frequency` = inf_post_frequency,
    `Influencer Engagement Growth Rate` = inf_engagement_growth_rate,
    `Influencer Average Reach` = inf_average_reach
  )

# Compute the correlation matrix
correlation_matrix <- cor(numeric_data, use = "complete.obs")

# Create the heatmap with a title and the renamed columns
pheatmap(correlation_matrix, 
         color = colorRampPalette(c("#FFF7BC", "#FDBB84", "#EF6548"))(50),  # Color palette
         display_numbers = TRUE,                                  # Show correlation values
         cluster_rows = FALSE,                                    # Disable row clustering
         cluster_cols = FALSE,                                    # Disable column clustering
         main = "Correlation Heatmap for Influencer Campaign Metrics") # Add a title
```
### Data Clustering
```{r}
# Select relevant columns for clustering (categorical and numeric)
clustering_data <- data %>%
  select(inf_influencer_tier, inf_audience_location, inf_audience_interests, inf_engagement_growth_rate)

# Preprocess categorical variables
clustering_data <- clustering_data %>%
  mutate(
    inf_influencer_tier = as.numeric(as.factor(inf_influencer_tier)),
    inf_audience_location = as.numeric(as.factor(inf_audience_location)),
    inf_audience_interests = as.numeric(as.factor(inf_audience_interests))
  )

# Scale the numeric variables to normalize
clustering_data <- clustering_data %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), scale))

# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(123)

# Perform K-Means clustering with 3 clusters 
kmeans_result <- kmeans(clustering_data, centers = 3, nstart = 25)

# Add the cluster information to the original dataset
data$cluster <- as.factor(kmeans_result$cluster)

# Descriptive Statistics for Each Cluster
# Get summary statistics for numeric variables in each cluster
summary_by_cluster <- data %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_engagement_growth = mean(inf_engagement_growth_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_follower_count = mean(inf_follower_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_campaign_cost = mean(cmp_campaign_cost, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_campaign_engagement = mean(cmp_total_engagement, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# View summary statistics for each cluster
View(summary_by_cluster)

# Visualizing the Clusters 
fviz_cluster(kmeans_result, data = clustering_data, 
             geom = "point",            
             stand = FALSE,             
             ellipse.type = "norm",     
             ggtheme = theme_minimal()) +
  labs(title = "K-Means Clustering of Influencers", 
       x = "Cluster", y = "Influencers") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 17, 15)) +  
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#FF6F61", "#33A1C9", "#FFD700")) + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```
```{r}
# Influencer Tier Distribution by Cluster 
ggplot(data, aes(x = inf_influencer_tier, fill = cluster)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Influencer Tier Distribution by Cluster", x = "Influencer Tier", y = "Count") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#FF6F61", "#33A1C9", "#FFD700")) + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

```{r}
# Audience Location Distribution by Cluster
ggplot(data, aes(x = inf_audience_location, fill = cluster)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Audience Location Distribution by Cluster", x = "Audience Location", y = "Count") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#FF6F61", "#33A1C9", "#FFD700")) + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

```{r}
# Platform Use Distribution by Cluster
ggplot(data, aes(x = cmp_platform_used, fill = cluster)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Platform Use Distribution by Cluster", x = "Platform", y = "Count") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#FF6F61", "#33A1C9", "#FFD700")) + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

### Machine-Learning Decision Tree Model
```{r}
# Assume a binary classification for high vs. low engagement
# Create a binary variable for high vs. low engagement
data$engagement_level <- ifelse(data$cmp_total_engagement > median(data$cmp_total_engagement), "High", "Low")

# Convert engagement_level to factor
data$engagement_level <- as.factor(data$engagement_level)

# Select relevant features for the model
model_data <- data %>% 
  select(engagement_level, inf_platform, inf_influencer_tier, cmp_campaign_type)

# Split data into training and test sets
set.seed(123)
trainIndex <- createDataPartition(model_data$engagement_level, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- model_data[trainIndex, ]
test_data <- model_data[-trainIndex, ]

# Train the Decision Tree
decision_tree <- rpart(engagement_level ~ ., data = train_data, 
                       parms = list(split = "gini"))

# Plot the Decision Tree with a title
rpart.plot(decision_tree, main = "Decision Tree for Engagement Level Prediction")

# Predict on test data
tree_predictions <- predict(decision_tree, test_data, type = "class")

# Confusion Matrix to evaluate the model
confusionMatrix(tree_predictions, test_data$engagement_level)

# Generate the confusion matrix
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(tree_predictions, test_data$engagement_level)

# Extract confusion matrix as a table
confusion_data <- as.data.frame(conf_matrix$table)

# Plot the confusion matrix using ggplot2
ggplot(data = confusion_data, aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), vjust = 1) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "#33A1C9") +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix (Accuracy: 0.838)", x = "Reference", y = "Prediction") + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```
